content.full_text:
Technical Assistance 
•• 
Administration •• 
Executive/legal 
(916) 322-5662 
322-5660 
322.5901 
Carole J. Roquemore 
Administrative Assistant 
Alta-Dena Certified Dairy 
17637 E. Valley Blvd. 
City of Industry, CA 
91747 
Dear Ms. Roquemore: 
May 14, 1984 
Re: 
FPPC No. A-84-l22 
Enforcement 
322-6«1 
This is in response to your letter of April 30, 1984, which 
asked whether the Alta-Dena Political Action Committee must report 
on its campaign disclosure statement (Form 420) a dinner provided 
to a legislator and his spouse and, if so, on what schedule would 
the expenditure be reported? 
Although the dinner does not constitute a "contribution" as 
defined by Section 82015 of the Political Re£orm Act (Government 
Code Sections 81000-91014), Section 84211 requires that recipient 
committees report all receipts and expenditures. 
Therefore, if PAC 
funds were used to pay the cost of the dinner, the expenditure must 
be reported on Schedule E (Payments) of the PAC's campaign 
disclosure statement. 
If the expenditure was made out of non-PAC 
funds, however, the PAC would not be required to report the 
activity. 
In addition, regardless of whether or not the expenditure was 
made out of the PAC's funds, if the Alta-Dena Certified Dairy is 
the employer of a lobbyist, the expenditure must be reported as an 
activity expense in Part 2, Section C of its Report of Lobbyist 
Employer (Form 650). 
I hope this information is helpful. 
Please call me at (916) 
322-5662 if you have any further questions. 
Sincerely, 
Carla J. Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 
CJW:kt 


17637 E. Valley Boulevard 
City 01 Industry. California 91747 
(818)964·6401 
April 30, 1984 
Jean Pritchard

Fair Political Practice Commission

ALTA-OENA 
Sacramento, CA

CERTIFIED DAIRY 
~ 
• 
"The dairy that 
cares about your health" 
Re: 
Form 420 
Recipient Committee Campaign Statement 
Dear Ms. Pritchard: 
On behalf of the Alta-Dena Political Action 
Committee, please respond to the following 
question: 
If a legislator and his wife, at our 
invitation, had dinner at a restaurant 
in Sacramento, would this expense be 
required to be reported on Form 420 and, 
if so, where?· 
~ 
.~ 
CAROLE J. ROQUE 
RE 
• Administrative ssistant

sections.conclusion: Although the dinner does not constitute a 'contribution' as defined by Section 82015 of the Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91014), Section 84211 requires that recipient committees report all receipts and expenditures. Therefore, if PAC funds were used to pay the cost of the dinner, the expenditure must be reported on Schedule E (Payments) of the PAC's campaign disclosure statement. If the expenditure was made out of non-PAC funds, however, the PAC would not be required to report the activity. In addition, regardless of whether or not the expenditure was made out of the PAC's funds, if the Alta-Dena Certified Dairy is the employer of a lobbyist, the expenditure must be reported as an activity expense in Part 2, Section C of its Report of Lobbyist Employer (Form 650).
sections.question_synthetic: Must a Political Action Committee report on Form 420 a dinner provided to a legislator and spouse, and if so, on which schedule?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: If PAC funds were used for the dinner, it must be reported on Schedule E of Form 420; if non-PAC funds were used, no PAC reporting is required. However, if Alta-Dena Certified Dairy is a lobbyist employer, the expenditure must be reported on Form 650 regardless of funding source.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Complete advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document includes both the incoming request (dated April 30, 1984) and FPPC response (dated May 14, 1984). FPPC No. A-84-122.
